blocks.body: Answers to questions (answers to questions) is an important aspect of the English language. Short answers with an auxiliary verb (Yes, I do / No, I don't) are usually used in conversation, rather than just "Yes" or "No". Full answers are used for greater clarity.

{ "title": "11. Short summary", "items": [ "Short answers is the standard way to answer Yes/No questions.", "Structure: Yes/No + Subject + Auxiliary (Yes, I do / No, I don't).", "With do/does/did: we repeat do/does/did in answers.", "With to be: repeat am/is/are/was/were in the answer.", "With modals: repeat the modal verb (can, will, should).", "With have: repeat have/has (have got, Present Perfect) information.", "Повні відповіді — для ясності або додаткової інформації.", "Уникаємо тільки "Yes" або "No" у формальному контексті.", "На Wh-питання НЕ відповідаємо Yes/No, а даємо конкретну інформацію.", "Combination: short answer + additional details = natural conversation." ] }
